_meta.description: REAL capture, converted to DetectionEvent shapes. Do not hand-edit the events without updating this file's source pointers below.
_meta.session: logged-in, watched twice ~5.5 min apart in the same spike session

sourceRecords.note: First load: 1 preroll + 14 midroll + 1 postroll placements (count 16) — RESULTS.md's '1/14/1'.
Midroll played at content time ~707s (RESULTS.md: 'midrolls played at 707s and 781s').

Reload ~5.5 min later: identical 14 midroll + 1 postroll placements but the preroll placement is GONE (count 15) — RESULTS.md's '0/14/1' and the exact rewatch-frequency-capping effect documented in RESULTS.md §3.5. This is the real-data case for classify()'s union-preroll/latest-midroll-count rule.
Midroll played at content time 781s, during the reload viewing.

_meta.eventOrderNote: The `events` array below is ordered by real capturedAt (first load PR -> first midroll DOM -> reload PR -> second midroll DOM), not grouped by source — the first DOM ad sits chronologically between the two player_response reads. classify() is unaffected: it takes the LAST item in ARRAY ORDER among PLAYER_RESPONSE-filtered events as 'latest', and the reload response (20:59:15.188Z) is genuinely the chronologically later of the two placement-bearing reads either way, so it remains last after filtering regardless of where the DOM events are interleaved.
omittedFromExport: A third full-set player_response pair at 2026-07-02T20:58:53.880Z (initial) / 20:58:58.066Z (getPlayerResponse), count 16, identical to the first load — redundant duplicate evidence, same rationale as the getPlayerResponse read below.

getPlayerResponse confirmation reads at 2026-07-02T20:53:43.808Z (count 16, identical adPlacements to the 20:53:39.572Z 'initial' read kept above) and 2026-07-02T20:58:58.066Z (part of the redundant reload pair above) — redundant duplicates of an already-kept placement-bearing read.

Fetch-path noise immediately following EACH 'initial'/'getPlayerResponse' read above (pageVideoId=__fmDj0ZJ1Q but a different/absent videoId): 2-3 records per read with videoId=null and note='no videoDetails' (malformed/prefetch reads with no videoDetails at all), one with videoId='ux' (2-character garbage from a non-canonical sibling endpoint — the /youtubei/v1/player/ad_break-class issue classifier.ts's summarizePlacementCounts doc comment describes), and one with an 11-character videoId that is an ad creative's OWN player response through the identical /youtubei/v1/player endpoint (dLCrtedz3dd at 20:53:39.627Z for the first load, W6kd21OkYpJ at 20:58:53.898Z for the omitted duplicate reload, c0xteAmUzvh at 20:59:15.423Z for the reload kept above). All real but exactly what the real pipeline drops: classify() and background.ts's handleIncomingEvent both filter on videoId !== pageVideoId (or a missing videoId) before an event would ever reach a session — omitted here for the identical reason, not fabricated absence.
Paired ad-interrupting-start events (millisecond-identical to the ad-showing-start timestamps above, spike-verified pairing) — redundant with the ad-showing-start events kept above.

ad-showing-end/ad-interrupting-end pairs for both midrolls (~707.3s and ~781s content time) — real but add no classification-relevant information here: since source A placements are present, classify() takes the placementsPresent branch and never calls typeDomEventsByContentTime (the only place DOM event kind/content-time matters); DOM evidence only needs to exist at all (domAdSeen = domAdEvents.length > 0) to add 'DOM' to sources, which the two ad-showing-start events already do.
conversionNotes: adPlacements[].offsetStartMs/offsetEndMs converted from the raw export's strings to numbers (see green-mrbeast-iYlODtkyw_I.json's conversionNotes for the same protobuf-JSON int64-as-string quirk).
No BEACON events captured by the Phase 0 spike tool (see green-mrbeast-iYlODtkyw_I.json).
_meta.expectedClassification: ADS_SERVED, preroll=true (from the first read, unioned across responses despite the reload dropping it), midrolls=14 (latest placement-bearing read), postroll=true, sources=[PLAYER_RESPONSE, DOM] — matches RESULTS.md §2 row 3.
